workshop_short_name: Geometry in the Age of Data-Driven Robotics
talk_title: Debate 1: Education (claim — mathematical foundations of geometry are a top priority for robotics education today)
speaker: Seth Hutchinson, Stefano Stramigioli vs Frank Park, Noémie Jaquier
tc_relevance_reason: Meta-discussion on whether classical geometric methods (Lie groups, screw theory) should still anchor robotics curricula in an ML-dominated era. Hutchinson and Park are co-authors of the two standard robotics textbooks. Useful context for TC educators but not technical content.

workshop_short_name: Geometry in the Age of Data-Driven Robotics
talk_title: Debate 2: Research (claim — the vast majority of interesting research questions on the role of geometry in robotics have already been answered)
speaker: Patrick Wensing, Antonio Franchi vs Andreas Müller, Georgia Chalvatzaki
speaker_affiliation: Notre Dame / Sapienza-Twente / JKU / TU Darmstadt
tc_relevance_reason: Pro/contra debate explicitly about whether geometric robotics research is exhausted. Four panelists whose work defines the TC's geometric methods (Wensing on whole-body dynamics, Franchi on aerial control, Müller on screw theory, Chalvatzaki bridging classical and learning) arguing about the future of TC's own foundations. Genuinely once-per-conference format.

workshop_short_name: Geometry in the Age of Data-Driven Robotics
talk_title: Debate 3: Communication (claim — formal mathematical jargon only serves to increase the field's barrier to entry)
speaker: Ross Hatton, Zachary Manchester vs Nadia Figueroa, Bruno Vilhena Adorno
tc_relevance_reason: Pro/contra debate on whether the math-heavy style of geometric robotics papers excludes potential contributors. Important meta-discussion but more about field culture than technical methodology.

workshop_short_name: Geometry in the Age of Data-Driven Robotics
talk_title: Full Panel Discussion (synthesis across all three debates)
speaker: Hutchinson, Stramigioli, Park, Jaquier, Wensing, Franchi, Müller, Chalvatzaki, Hatton, Manchester, Figueroa, Adorno
tc_relevance_reason: All twelve panelists in one room synthesizing the day's debates on geometric methods in robotics. Pulls together TC senior figures from kinematics (Park), dynamics (Wensing, Manchester), control (Stramigioli, Franchi), geometric mechanics (Hatton, Adorno) and learning (Jaquier, Chalvatzaki, Figueroa) at the same table. Closing community moment of the workshop day.

workshop_short_name: AI-Driven Safe Aerial Robotics
speaker: Nora Ayanian
tc_relevance_reason: Collision avoidance for multiple aerial vehicles is a core motion-planning problem. Ayanian's group works on decentralized multi-robot coordination algorithms — exactly the kind of distributed planning the TC cares about.

workshop_short_name: AI-Driven Safe Aerial Robotics
talk_title: Aggressive acrobatics in insect-scale aerial robots via deep-learned model predictive control
speaker: Kevin Chen
tc_relevance_reason: Title is explicit: deep-learned MPC for aggressive flight on insect-scale robots. Sits at the intersection of learning-augmented control and constrained real-time optimization on extremely limited compute — both TC priorities.

workshop_short_name: AI-Driven Safe Aerial Robotics
talk_title: Toward Physical AI: Autonomy, Collaboration, and Language-Guided Behavior in Robotic Systems
speaker: Vijay Kumar
tc_relevance_reason: Kumar is a foundational figure in aerial robotics — multi-robot coordination, agile flight, swarm control. The title here is broad (vision talk) but Kumar's perspective on where autonomy is going for physical AI is worth hearing for any TC member working on multi-robot or aerial planning.

workshop_short_name: AI-Driven Safe Aerial Robotics
speaker: Elia Kaufmann
tc_relevance_reason: Kaufmann is known for the UZH champion-level drone racing work; now at Skydio. Visual navigation is TC-adjacent — the work produces flight controllers but the framing here is perception-to-policy rather than planning/control algorithm design.

workshop_short_name: AI-Driven Safe Aerial Robotics
speaker: Daniele Palossi
tc_relevance_reason: Onboard ML for tiny drones (nano-drones). Mostly about deploying learned policies on extremely constrained MCUs; relevant context for TC members thinking about real-time computation budgets.

workshop_short_name: AI-Driven Safe Aerial Robotics
talk_title: De-risking Autonomy: Leonardo's approach to safe and reliable autonomous systems
speaker: Fabrizio Schiano
tc_relevance_reason: Industry safety engineering perspective from a major aerospace company. Useful real-world deployment context but not a TC algorithmic contribution.

workshop_short_name: AI-Driven Safe Aerial Robotics
talk_title: Bio-inspired AI for inherently safe, lightweight flying robots
speaker: Guido de Croon
tc_relevance_reason: Bio-inspired control for tiny flying robots. De Croon's lab does some genuine control work on MAVs; this specific talk's framing is bio-inspired AI which is more design-philosophy than control methodology.

workshop_short_name: Uncertainty in Open-World Robotics
speaker: Marija Popović
tc_relevance_reason: Popović works on informative path planning — choosing UAV trajectories that maximize information gain for environmental monitoring. Direct TC scope: planning under uncertainty with information-theoretic objectives. Talk title not yet published but her core research area is squarely TC.

workshop_short_name: Uncertainty in Open-World Robotics
talk_title: Scalable Multi-Agent Navigation: Hierarchical Planning Under Real-World Uncertainty
speaker: Nicholas Roy
tc_relevance_reason: Nick Roy is the senior figure in planning under uncertainty / POMDP-based navigation. His group has produced foundational work on belief-space planning and motion planning under uncertainty. The title literally says 'Hierarchical Planning Under Real-World Uncertainty' — bullseye TC content from the leading voice in the subfield. Run across the convention for this one.

workshop_short_name: Scaling Compositional Intelligence for Multi-Agent Robotic Systems
speaker: Marco Pavone
tc_relevance_reason: Pavone's group does multi-robot motion planning, decentralized control, and AV decision-making in the classical TC tradition. For a multi-agent workshop, his contribution is the most planning-and-control-flavored.

workshop_short_name: Scaling Compositional Intelligence for Multi-Agent Robotic Systems
speaker: Xu Liu
tc_relevance_reason: Stanford MSL (Mac Schwager's Multi-robot Systems Lab) — decentralized control and multi-robot motion coordination. Direct TC scope: multi-robot motion planning algorithms.

workshop_short_name: Scaling Compositional Intelligence for Multi-Agent Robotic Systems
speaker: Chelsea Finn
tc_relevance_reason: Co-leads Physical Intelligence; pioneer in imitation learning and meta-learning for robot policies. Highly influential but builds learned policies rather than planning/control algorithms — TC-adjacent.

workshop_short_name: Scaling Compositional Intelligence for Multi-Agent Robotic Systems
speaker: Eugene Vinitsky
tc_relevance_reason: Multi-agent RL for autonomous driving and traffic. Multi-agent RL produces multi-agent policies but doesn't advance multi-agent planning algorithms in the classical sense — TC-adjacent.

workshop_short_name: Xplore: Cross-Disciplinary aspects of Exploration in Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, and Search
speaker: Steve LaValle
tc_relevance_reason: LaValle wrote 'Planning Algorithms' (the standard motion-planning textbook) and co-invented the RRT — foundational to most modern sampling-based motion planners. He moved to VR research years ago and rarely speaks at ICRA. Hearing him on exploration is run-across-the-convention rare.

workshop_short_name: Xplore: Cross-Disciplinary aspects of Exploration in Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, and Search
speaker: Jen Jen Chung
tc_relevance_reason: Chung works on informative path planning and multi-robot exploration — squarely TC scope (planning algorithms that gather information).

workshop_short_name: Xplore: Cross-Disciplinary aspects of Exploration in Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, and Search
speaker: Bakir Lacevic
tc_relevance_reason: Lacevic works on path planning and configuration space topology — classical TC territory (sampling-based / topological motion planning).

workshop_short_name: Xplore: Cross-Disciplinary aspects of Exploration in Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, and Search
speaker: Peter Auer
tc_relevance_reason: Auer is a foundational RL theorist (UCB algorithm, regret bounds). Theoretical RL is TC-adjacent — useful background for understanding exploration in learning-augmented planners but not directly motion-planning methodology.

workshop_short_name: Xplore: Cross-Disciplinary aspects of Exploration in Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, and Search
speaker: Antonin Raffin
tc_relevance_reason: Raffin maintains Stable-Baselines3 (the standard open-source RL library). Useful infrastructure for TC members training learned policies but not TC algorithm design.

workshop_short_name: Xplore: Cross-Disciplinary aspects of Exploration in Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, and Search
speaker: Jens Kober
tc_relevance_reason: Kober works on robot learning from demonstration, motion primitives, and policy search. TC-adjacent (LfD generates motion policies but doesn't advance planning/control algorithms in the classical sense).

workshop_short_name: Xplore: Cross-Disciplinary aspects of Exploration in Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, and Search
speaker: Sebastian Tschiatschek
tc_relevance_reason: Tschiatschek works on ML theory, active learning, decision-making under uncertainty. TC-adjacent through the active-learning-for-planning angle.

workshop_short_name: Workshop on Manipulation Robustness: Towards Human-Level Robustness under Real-World Challenges
speaker: Andrea Bajcsy
tc_relevance_reason: Bajcsy's group works on safe control with human models, reachability-based safety, and value-aware planning. Direct TC scope: safe motion planning and control under uncertainty.

workshop_short_name: Workshop on Manipulation Robustness: Towards Human-Level Robustness under Real-World Challenges
speaker: Jan Peters
tc_relevance_reason: Peters is a foundational figure in robot learning (policy search, learning from demonstration). His older work touches control theory but recent output is mostly learning-flavored — TC-adjacent.

workshop_short_name: Workshop on Manipulation Robustness: Towards Human-Level Robustness under Real-World Challenges
speaker: Oliver Brock
tc_relevance_reason: Brock's RBO Hand and interactive perception work treats perception+action as a single problem. TC-adjacent — useful for active-sensing-and-control formulations.

workshop_short_name: 8th Workshop on Long-Term Human Motion Prediction
speaker: Katherine Driggs-Campbell
tc_relevance_reason: Driggs-Campbell's group works on social navigation — planning around predicted human motion. Closest TC angle in this prediction-focused workshop.

workshop_short_name: 8th Workshop on Long-Term Human Motion Prediction
speaker: Jean Oh
tc_relevance_reason: Oh works on social navigation and learning-from-experience policies for robots in human environments. TC-adjacent — planning around predicted motion is a TC consumer of prediction.

workshop_short_name: 8th Workshop on Long-Term Human Motion Prediction
speaker: Johannes Betz
tc_relevance_reason: Betz's group does autonomous racing and AV planning/control (also organizes Monday's GenAV). TC-adjacent — predicted human/agent motion feeds into AV planners.

workshop_short_name: Robots Meet Prior Maps: Leveraging Geometric and Semantic Priors for Real-World Robot Autonomy
speaker: David Hsu
tc_relevance_reason: Hsu is the senior figure in POMDP-based planning and planning under uncertainty (DESPOT, the standard online POMDP solver). Bullseye TC. Also giving Keynote 5 on Thursday.

workshop_short_name: Robots Meet Prior Maps: Leveraging Geometric and Semantic Priors for Real-World Robot Autonomy
speaker: Jen Jen Chung
tc_relevance_reason: Same speaker as Xplore — informative path planning and exploration. Direct TC scope.

workshop_short_name: Robots Meet Prior Maps: Leveraging Geometric and Semantic Priors for Real-World Robot Autonomy
speaker: Jose-Luis Sanchez-Lopez
tc_relevance_reason: Sanchez-Lopez works on UAV planning and control. TC-adjacent.

workshop_short_name: Semantics for Reliable Robot Autonomy: From Environment Understanding and Reasoning to Safe Interaction
talk_title: Reasoning VLA Models for Vehicle Autonomy
speaker: Marco Pavone
tc_relevance_reason: Pavone giving a talk specifically on VLA models for vehicle autonomy — more on the learning-applied-to-AVs side than his usual planning/control work. TC-adjacent.

workshop_short_name: Semantics for Reliable Robot Autonomy: From Environment Understanding and Reasoning to Safe Interaction
talk_title: LLM-Enabled Robots: Jailbreaking Attacks and Defenses
speaker: George Pappas
tc_relevance_reason: Pappas is a major control-theoretic figure (control of cyber-physical systems, formal methods). This specific talk is on adversarial robustness of LLM-driven robot policies — an emerging interface between his control theory work and modern learned policies. TC-adjacent.

workshop_short_name: Semantics for Reliable Robot Autonomy: From Environment Understanding and Reasoning to Safe Interaction
talk_title: Building an Adaptable Generalist Robot: A Human-Centered Perspective
speaker: Mengdi Xu
tc_relevance_reason: Xu works on safe RL and generalist robot adaptation. TC-adjacent through the safe-learning angle.

workshop_short_name: Semantics for Reliable Robot Autonomy: From Environment Understanding and Reasoning to Safe Interaction
talk_title: Semantics for Robot Task Execution Monitoring
speaker: Dongheui Lee
tc_relevance_reason: Lee's group does robot learning + task execution monitoring. TC-adjacent — execution monitoring is the runtime side of planning/control.
